title: BME280 Environmental Sensor
desc: Precision I2C environmental sensor that measures temperature, humidity, and barometric pressure for ESP32 weather, altitude, and climate projects.

title: DHT22 Temperature & Humidity Sensor
desc: Digital temperature and humidity sensor for ESP32 weather stations, room monitors, greenhouse controllers, and climate automation projects.

title: ESP32-CAM (AI-Thinker)
desc: AI-Thinker ESP32-CAM board with an integrated OV2640 camera, fixed camera pin map, FTDI upload wiring, 5 V power guidance, and privacy-first local camera use.

title: ESP32 DevKit Board
desc: Beginner-friendly ESP32 development board with USB programming, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, 3.3 V GPIO, and enough pins for real hardware projects.

title: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Distance Sensor
desc: Low-cost ultrasonic distance sensor that measures nearby objects with sound pulses for ESP32 robots, parking aids, and level sensing.

title: HC-SR501 PIR Motion Sensor
desc: Passive infrared motion sensor module that gives an ESP32 a digital HIGH signal when warm objects move through its field of view.

title: 1-Channel Relay Module
desc: Electrically isolated switch module that lets an ESP32 control higher-power DC loads and carefully supervised AC loads from a low-current GPIO signal.

title: SSD1306 OLED Display
desc: Small monochrome I2C OLED display for showing ESP32 sensor readings, menus, status messages, icons, and debugging information.

title: Analog Inputs: Reading the Real World
desc: Buttons teach the ESP32 yes or no. Analog inputs teach it how much: how bright, how loud, how wet, how far, or where a dial is turned.

title: Analog Inputs
desc: Buttons teach the ESP32 yes or no. Analog inputs teach it how much: how bright, how loud, how wet, how far, or where a dial is turned.

title: Blink an LED with ESP32
desc: Your first real ESP32 project — one LED, a few wires, and code you write yourself. Let's make it blink!

title: Button Controls LED with ESP32
desc: Make your ESP32 react to your hand. In this mission a push button becomes an input, an LED becomes the output, and your code connects cause to effect.

title: Connect OLED Display with ESP32
desc: Serial Monitor is great for debugging — but a real screen on your project looks amazing. This mission adds a tiny OLED display that shows your own messages.

title: Button Debouncing
desc: Your button now has a stable default state. The next problem is speed: the ESP32 can see tiny mechanical bounces that your finger cannot feel.

title: Understanding Digital Inputs and Floating Pins
desc: Your button worked in Mission 02. Now you will learn the electronics behind it: why an input pin needs a clear HIGH or LOW, and why an unconnected pin can behave randomly.

title: Reading Environmental Sensors
desc: Embedded systems become useful when they observe the physical world. In this mission, your ESP32 reads air conditions and shows them on a local OLED screen.

title: I2C Communication
desc: I2C is the reason one ESP32 can talk to a display, a sensor, and other smart modules using the same two signal wires.

title: Installing Arduino IDE for ESP32: Complete Setup Guide
desc: Before you wire sensors or publish Wi-Fi dashboards, your computer must recognize the ESP32 DevKit and compile firmware with the correct board package. This guide walks through a complete Arduino IDE setup on Windows, macOS, and Linux—from download to a verified blink upload.

title: Multiple Buttons & State Detection
desc: One debounced button is useful. Real projects often need several buttons, and each one needs its own clean state, timing, and purpose.

title: OLED Display with ESP32
desc: Serial Monitor is useful while your ESP32 is connected to a computer. An OLED display lets your project explain itself without needing the Arduino IDE open.

title: Pull-up vs Pull-down Resistors
desc: You saw in Mission 03 that a floating input can behave randomly. Now you will learn the two simple resistor patterns that give every button a reliable default state.

title: PWM Fundamentals
desc: Digital pins can switch fully ON or fully OFF. PWM teaches the ESP32 to create useful in-between effects such as dim light and slower motor speed.

title: Read Temperature with DHT22
desc: Build a real ESP32 room sensor with the DHT22. You will wire power, ground, and one data pin, then read temperature and humidity in Arduino Serial Monitor.

title: Reading Analog Sensors
desc: A potentiometer is a dial you control by hand. Analog sensors use the same ADC idea, but the real world turns the dial for you.

title: Smart Environment Monitor
desc: This final Foundation mission combines sensors, displays, buttons, GPIO, decisions, and debugging into one complete embedded systems project.

title: What Is ESP32? Complete Beginner's Guide
desc: ESP32 is a low-cost, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth capable microcontroller that lets you build connected sensors, automation, and IoT devices without a separate computer on every node. This guide explains how it works, where it fits in your toolkit, and how to start your first project safely.

title: Build an Isolated AC Current Monitor Prototype
desc: Build a bounded ESP32 AC current monitor prototype with an SCT-013 clamp, ADC biasing, calibration limits, and strict mains-safety guidance.

title: Build an ESP32-CAM Color Object Detector Demo
desc: Build a bounded ESP32-CAM color object presence demo with OV2640 RGB565 frames, privacy notes, and no unsupported AI claims.

title: ESP32 Air Quality Monitor
desc: An ESP32 monitor that reads an MQ135 gas sensor, prints live raw values, and drives an alert output or fan relay when air quality crosses a tested threshol

title: ESP32 BLE Beacon
desc: A no-extra-hardware ESP32 project that advertises a BLE beacon packet and lets you detect it from a phone app. Includes wiring, code, troubleshooting, safe

title: Build an ESP32-CAM Face Detection Privacy Demo
desc: Build a local ESP32-CAM face detection demo that distinguishes detection from recognition, with privacy, lighting, memory, and power limits.

title: ESP32-CAM QR Code Scanner
desc: An ESP32-CAM scanner that captures frames and decodes QR codes for access demos, inventory labels, or classroom identification projects. Includes wiring, c

title: ESP32 Camera Capture Server
desc: An ESP32-CAM project that captures images from the OV2640 camera and serves or stores them for inspection, timelapse, or simple monitoring. Includes wiring

title: Build a Low-Voltage CNC Motion Controller Demo
desc: Build a bounded ESP32 CNC motion-control demo with A4988 STEP/DIR wiring, separate motor power, current-limit setup, and safety limits.

title: ESP32 Digital Piano
desc: Build an ESP32 digital piano with capacitive touch keys, I2S audio output, and MIDI over USB. From a simple buzzer piano to a full polyphonic synthesiser with waveform selection.

title: ESP32 Distance Monitoring System
desc: Measure distance with an HC-SR04 on ESP32 using TRIG, ECHO through a voltage divider, and a buzzer alert.

title: Build an Educational ECG Waveform Viewer
desc: Build an educational ESP32 AD8232 waveform viewer with ADC sampling, leads-off checks, battery/isolation guidance, and no medical claims.

title: ESP32 Fire Alarm System
desc: Build an ESP32 fire alarm with MQ-2 smoke sensor and DHT22 temperature sensor. Covers local buzzer alerts, OLED display, and MQTT smart-home integration.

title: Build an APDS-9960 Gesture Control Demo
desc: Build a technically honest ESP32 gesture demo with APDS-9960 I2C wiring, interrupt handling, LED outputs, and false-trigger limits.

title: Build a Local NEO-6M GPS Reader
desc: Build an ESP32 NEO-6M GPS reader with UART wiring, TinyGPS++ parsing, no-fix handling, sky-view guidance, and location privacy limits.

title: Build a Low-Voltage Greenhouse Vent Controller
desc: Build a safe ESP32 greenhouse ventilation prototype with DHT22 readings, OLED status, relay wiring, hysteresis, and low-voltage limits.

title: ESP32 Home Climate Automation
desc: Build a room climate controller that reads environmental data and automatically controls a fan, vent, or humidifier indicator.

title: ESP32 IoT Weather Station
desc: Build an ESP32 weather station that measures temperature, humidity, and pressure, then reports clean readings for dashboards or local displays.

title: Build an ESP32 IR Learning Remote Demo
desc: Build an ESP32 IR remote demo with TSOP38238 receive wiring, transistor-driven IR LED output, IRremote library code, and compatibility limits.

title: ESP32 Learning Trainer Board
desc: Classroom ESP32 trainer with a push button on GPIO12 and status LED on GPIO14.

title: ESP32 LED Matrix Display
desc: A scrolling LED message display using an ESP32 and a MAX7219 matrix module, with clean SPI wiring and brightness control. Includes wiring, code, troublesho

title: Build an Educational Lightning Sensor Demo
desc: Build an ESP32 AS3935 lightning sensor demo with SPI wiring, interrupt handling, noise/disturber classification, and official-alert safety limits.

title: ESP32 Line Following Robot
desc: Build a two-motor robot that reads line sensors and steers itself along a dark track.

title: ESP32 LoRa Remote Sensor Node (Point-to-Point)
desc: Build a two-node ESP32 LoRa sensor link with real point-to-point radio, legal frequency checks, antenna safety, and packet validation.

title: ESP32 Machine Monitoring Node
desc: Production-ready walkthrough — Current Sensor signal on GPIO35 drives Relay Output control with a tuned threshold sketch you can deploy today.

title: ESP32 Motion Security Alert
desc: Build a motion alert node that detects movement with a PIR sensor and triggers a buzzer, LED, or Wi-Fi alert.

title: ESP32 MQTT Sensor Dashboard
desc: An ESP32 sensor node that publishes temperature and humidity readings to an MQTT broker so dashboards such as Node-RED or Home Assistant can display live d

title: ESP32 NeoPixel Music Visualizer (Amplitude-Reactive)
desc: Build a real amplitude-reactive NeoPixel visualizer with an I2S digital microphone, external LED power, and honest no-FFT scope.

title: ESP32 OLED Weather Clock
desc: Build a Wi-Fi clock that shows NTP time and weather-style readings on a crisp OLED display.

title: Build an Educational Pulse Oximeter Logger
desc: Build an educational ESP32 MAX30102 logger with SDA GPIO21, SCL GPIO22, estimated-only output, no-finger suppression, and medical-use warnings.

title: ESP32 RFID Access Control System
desc: Build an RFID access controller that reads cards, compares IDs, and unlocks a low-voltage output for approved tags.

title: ESP32 RFID Inventory Tracker
desc: Build an ESP32 RFID inventory tracker using the RC522 module. Scan asset tags, log check-in and check-out events to SD card, and monitor stock levels from a live web dashboard.

title: ESP32 RGB LED Pattern Controller
desc: Learn PWM color mixing with a single common-cathode RGB LED and a button-driven non-blocking pattern state machine on ESP32.

title: ESP32 Educational Servo Arm Controller (3-DOF)
desc: Build a small joystick-controlled 3-DOF servo arm on ESP32 with safe angle limits, incremental motion, and separate servo power.

title: ESP32-CAM Local Network Camera Demo
desc: Build a local ESP32-CAM camera page with AI-Thinker pin mapping, stable 5 V power, placeholder Wi-Fi credentials, and privacy-first limits.

title: ESP32 Smart Door Lock
desc: Build a safe smart lock prototype using ESP32, an access input, and a low-voltage lock actuator output.

title: ESP32 Low-Voltage Energy Meter Demo
desc: Build a low-voltage ESP32 energy-measurement demonstration using ADC readings and relative power calculations. Not billing grade or for direct mains.

title: ESP32 Smart Irrigation System
desc: Build a plant watering controller that reads soil moisture and switches a pump or valve only when the soil is dry.

title: ESP32 Smart Mailbox
desc: Build an ESP32 smart mailbox that detects mail delivery with a reed switch and sends Telegram notifications. Covers basic alert, ESP-NOW indoor relay, and deep-sleep battery operation.

title: ESP32 Smart Parking Sensor
desc: A parking-bay detector that measures distance with an HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor and uses LEDs or a dashboard output to show whether a space is free or occu

title: Build a Low-Voltage Smart Power Strip Simulator
desc: Build a low-voltage ESP32 smart power strip simulator with MOSFET outputs on GPIO25, GPIO26, GPIO27, GPIO33 and no mains wiring.

title: ESP32 Smart Street Light
desc: A light-sensing ESP32 controller that turns a lamp output on at night and can use motion sensing to brighten only when movement is detected. Includes wirin

title: ESP32 Smart Thermostat
desc: Build a safe low-voltage thermostat prototype that reads room temperature and switches a fan or indicator when a threshold is crossed.

title: ESP32 Soil Moisture Monitor
desc: A plant monitor that reads a capacitive soil sensor, converts raw ADC values into a moisture percentage, and uses LEDs or a buzzer to show when watering is

title: Build an Educational Soil pH Monitor
desc: Build an educational ESP32 soil pH monitor with a conditioning board, GPIO34 ADC1 input, pH 4/pH 7 calibration, and no fertilizer advice.

title: ESP32 UV Index Monitor (Educational Estimate)
desc: Build an educational UV-index monitor with a real VEML6075 I2C UV sensor on ESP32, clearly labeled as an estimate and not medical guidance.

title: ESP32 Vibration Monitor
desc: Build an ESP32 vibration monitor with MPU6050 accelerometer. From threshold alerts to FFT spectrum analysis and MQTT predictive maintenance dashboards.

title: ESP32 Sound-Triggered Relay Demo
desc: Build an ESP32 sound-triggered relay demo with an INMP441 I2S microphone, peak detection, and a low-voltage relay load. Not speech recognition.

title: ESP32 Water Leak Detector
desc: A low-voltage water leak alarm that detects moisture on a sensor strip, sounds a buzzer, and can later send alerts over Wi-Fi. Includes wiring, code, troub

title: ESP32 WiFi Robot Controller
desc: Build a browser-controlled ESP32 robot that drives motors from Wi-Fi commands served by the board itself.
